content.problemStatement:
Rockeater Rocky is on a mission to eat rocks!

There are NNN rocks on the XXX-axis. The ithi^{th}ith of these rocks is at position XiX_iXi‚Äã and has weight WiW_iWi‚Äã.

All the WiW_iWi‚Äã are **distinct**.

Consider a subset SSS consisting of kkk rocks. Rocky will eat the rocks in SSS as follows:


Let i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iki_1, i_2, \ldots, i_ki1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ik‚Äã be the list of indices of rocks in SSS, sorted in **ascending** order of weight.

That is, Wi1<Wi2<‚Ä¶<WikW_{i_1} \lt W_{i_2} \lt \ldots \lt W_{i_k}Wi1‚Äã‚Äã<Wi2‚Äã‚Äã<‚Ä¶<Wik‚Äã‚Äã


Rocky will eat the rocks in SSS in ascending order of weight. That is, Rocky will start at position Xi1X_{i_1}Xi1‚Äã‚Äã and eat the rock there; then move to position Xi2X_{i_2}Xi2‚Äã‚Äã and eat the rock there, and so on till all the rocks in SSS are eaten.
The distance traveled by Rocky is thus ‚à£Xi1‚àíXi2‚à£+‚à£Xi2‚àíXi3‚à£+‚Ä¶+‚à£Xik‚àí1‚àíXik‚à£|X_{i_1} - X_{i_2}| + |X_{i_2} - X_{i_3}| + \ldots + |X_{i_{k-1}} - X_{i_k}|‚à£Xi1‚Äã‚Äã‚àíXi2‚Äã‚Äã‚à£+‚à£Xi2‚Äã‚Äã‚àíXi3‚Äã‚Äã‚à£+‚Ä¶+‚à£Xik‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã‚àíXik‚Äã‚Äã‚à£.
In particular, if ‚à£S‚à£‚â§1|S| \leq 1‚à£S‚à£‚â§1 the distance traveled by Rocky is 000.
Rocky hasn't decided yet which subset of rocks to eat.

If every subset is equally likely to be chosen, find the **expected value** of the distance traveled by Rocky, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

More formally, under the constraints of the problem, it can be proved that the expected value can be represented as a rational number, i.e, an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äã‚Äã.
Further, there exists a unique integer RRR such that R√óQ‚â°P(mod109+7)R\times Q \equiv P \pmod {10^9 + 7}R√óQ‚â°P(mod109+7) and 0‚â§R<109+70\leq R \lt 10^9+70‚â§R<109+7. Print this integer RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of rocks.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äã,X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äã ‚Äî the positions of the rocks.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers W1,W2,‚Ä¶,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1‚Äã,W2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,WN‚Äã ‚Äî the weights of the rocks.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the expected distance traveled by Rocky, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are four possible subsets Rocky can choose. Three of them ({},{1},{5}\{\}, \{1\}, \{5\}{},{1},{5}) require no travel, while in the fourth ({1,5})(\{1, 5\})({1,5}), Rocky has to travel a distance of ‚à£5‚àí1‚à£=4|5 - 1| = 4‚à£5‚àí1‚à£=4.
The expected distance traveled is hence the average of 0+0+0+40 + 0 + 0 + 40+0+0+4, which is 111.
Test case 222: There are 888 possible subsets of rocks. Four of them have a travel distance of 000. For the rest:

{1,20}\{1, 20\}{1,20} has a travel distance of 191919.
{1,34}\{1, 34\}{1,34} has a travel distance of 333333.
{20,34}\{20, 34\}{20,34} has a travel distance of 141414.
{1,20,34}\{1, 20, 34\}{1,20,34} has a travel distance of 474747, because Rocky moves 1‚Üí34‚Üí201 \to 34 \to 201‚Üí34‚Üí20 in ascending order of rock weights.

The answer is thus the average of 0+0+0+0+19+14+33+47=1130 + 0 + 0 + 0 + 19 + 14 + 33 + 47 = 1130+0+0+0+19+14+33+47=113, and 1138\frac{113}{8}8113‚Äã modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7 is 125000015125000015125000015.
